message:
chore(beads): close P0 attention feed validation gate br-7h8it

Validation complete:
- go build passes
- go test -short passes (assign, swarm, tui, webhook verified)
- e2e tests pass (all operator loop scenarios)
- Capabilities output shows attention feed commands

Bug fixes verified working:
- Channel lifecycle (limit_detector, auto_respawner, webhook manager)
- Nil safety in effectiveness scoring
- Race condition fix in dashboard layout tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-6tzh9","title":"Attention Feed Phase 3b: Ship --robot-digest as the default what-changed surface","description":"# Why this bead exists
`--robot-digest` should become the default answer to "what changed since I last looked?" for operator agents that do not need the full raw feed every time. This bead turns the digest engine into a polished robot-mode command.

# Scope
1. Add a canonical `--robot-digest` command with cursor-based replay semantics.
2. Return `cursor_start`, `cursor_end`, `summary`, digest buckets, and suppression counts.
3. Support the same filtering philosophy as the raw feed while keeping the surface simpler than full event inspection.
4. Return explicit resync guidance when the requested cursor is too old.
5. Ensure the command points naturally to `--robot-events` or more targeted robot surfaces for follow-up when needed.
6. Keep response shape stable enough that operator agents can rely on it as the default steady-state loop surface.

# Detailed verification expectations
- Golden tests for normal digest output and expired-cursor behavior.
- Tests that digest filters line up with the underlying engine.
- Tests proving the command can surface both raw-state churn suppression and high-value signals.
- Transcript-style scenario coverage for: snapshot -> digest -> attention -> digest follow-up.
- Detailed logs in tests that print summary text, bucket counts, suppression counts, and cursor handoff when expectations fail.

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `--robot-digest` exists and is useful as the default delta surface.
- [ ] The response shape makes cursor chaining and follow-up inspection obvious.
- [ ] Coordination-aware and core attention signals both appear correctly once available.
- [ ] The command reduces the need for repeated full snapshots in normal operator loops.
- [ ] Digest transcript scenarios are covered with detailed logs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:20.926802931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T08:41:00.847819208Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T08:41:00.847252827Z","close_reason":"DigestOptions, DigestResponse, PrintDigest implemented in events.go with tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["attention-feed","cli","digest","phase-3","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-6tzh9","depends_on_id":"br-3qs46","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:40.989461011Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-6tzh9","depends_on_id":"br-815ez","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:24.498489127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-6tzh9","depends_on_id":"br-89wvt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:42.881169266Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-6tzh9","depends_on_id":"br-fsoxa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:24.805353019Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-6tzh9","depends_on_id":"br-kpvhy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:25.130344370Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-6tzh9","depends_on_id":"br-vdfjr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:40.607667325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-7746h","title":"Attention Feed Phase 4b0: Build shared Go scenario harness, tmux fixtures, and artifact plumbing for transcript and e2e verification","description":"# Why this bead exists
Both the core attention-feed initiative and the overlay-feed integration branch now require Go-based end-to-end/transcript verification with rich artifacts. If each branch invents its own tmux fixture lifecycle, artifact pathing, timeout policy, and logging format, the result will be duplicated, inconsistent, and much harder to debug. This bead creates one shared Go verification harness so the later transcript/e2e beads can focus on scenarios instead of re-building plumbing.

# Scope
1. Build reusable Go helpers for tmux-backed scenario setup/teardown, including deterministic session naming, cleanup on failure, and explicit timeout handling.
2. Define one shared artifact strategy for transcript/e2e runs: directory layout, naming, timestamp format, retention/debug mode, and where stdout/stderr, cursor traces, rendered summaries, and transport captures should be written.
3. Provide reusable helpers for invoking ntm robot/CLI/network surfaces, capturing structured output, and annotating artifact timelines with scenario steps.
4. Provide reusable assertion/logging helpers for cursor handoff, wake reasons, degraded markers, focus targets, and other operator-loop invariants that multiple e2e beads will need.
5. Keep the harness implemented in Go and suitable for use from `e2e/` and integration-style tests; do not rely on shell-script runners as the canonical infrastructure.
6. Make the harness neutral enough that both the core attention-feed transcript bead and the overlay-feed transcript bead can reuse it without forking conventions.

# Design considerations
- The harness should reduce duplication, not become a mini framework that obscures what each scenario is doing.
- Cleanup must be robust enough that failed tmux-based tests do not leave session clutter behind.
- Artifact layout should optimize post-failure diagnosis first, not just “green test” terseness.
- Shared helpers must preserve enough raw detail that later failures can still be understood without stepping through the harness internals.

# Detailed verification expectations
- Unit tests for artifact path generation, timestamp formatting, retention/debug behavior, and cleanup helpers.
- Tests for helper behavior when tmux setup fails, command execution fails, or cleanup itself encounters missing resources.
- Example harness-driven smoke scenarios that prove artifacts are emitted in the documented structure.
- Detailed `t.Logf` output in harness tests showing artifact root, session IDs, cleanup decisions, and retained artifact locations when assertions fail.

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A reusable Go scenario/artifact harness exists for transcript/e2e work.
- [ ] Both the core attention-feed and overlay-feed e2e/transcript beads can depend on it instead of inventing separate plumbing.
- [ ] Artifact layout, retention/debug mode, and cleanup behavior are explicit and tested.
- [ ] The harness improves diagnosability without forcing shell-script orchestration into the repo.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-21T01:23:46.362083521Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T02:43:08.191993090Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T02:43:08.191497012Z","close_reason":"Implemented shared Go scenario harness with: ArtifactManager for standardized artifact layout, ScenarioHarness for tmux-backed e2e, RobotResult with JSON helpers, CursorTraceEntry for cursor tracking, assertion helpers. All unit tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["attention-feed","e2e","harness","overlay","tests","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-7746h","depends_on_id":"br-aa0nj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:25:31.177950401Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-7dnb7","title":"Phase 3 VERIFY: Run Full TUI Inspector Suite — Structural Refactor Gate","description":"# Phase 3 Verification Gate

## Purpose
After all Phase 3 tasks complete, verify no regressions.

## Mandatory Steps

### 1. Build verification
  go build ./cmd/ntm 2>&1 | tee /tmp/gate3_build.log
  go vet ./internal/tui/... 2>&1 | tee /tmp/gate3_vet.log

### 2. Unit test regression check
  go test -v -count=1 -short ./internal/tui/... 2>&1 | tee /tmp/gate3_tests.log
  # Count passing tests
  PASS_COUNT=$(grep -c '^--- PASS' /tmp/gate3_tests.log)
  BASELINE_COUNT=$(grep -c '^--- PASS' /tmp/tui_test_baseline.log)
  echo "Tests: phase3=$PASS_COUNT baseline=$BASELINE_COUNT"
  # PASS_COUNT must be >= BASELINE_COUNT (may be higher due to new tests)
  if [ "$PASS_COUNT" -lt "$BASELINE_COUNT" ]; then
      echo "ERROR: Test count regression! $PASS_COUNT < $BASELINE_COUNT"
      exit 1
  fi

### 3. VHS capture suite
  bash /dp/tui_inspector/scripts/run_ntm_tui_suite.sh \
    --profiles ntm-dashboard-empty,ntm-dashboard-help,ntm-dashboard-navigate,ntm-dashboard-wide,ntm-palette \
    --suite-name "phase3_post_upgrade"

### 4. Snapshot comparison
  For each profile, compare phase3 vs previous phase snapshots.
  Log any visual differences.

### 5. Log summary
  Create /tmp/gate3_summary.json:
  {
    "phase": 3,
    "build": "pass|fail",
    "vet": "pass|fail",
    "test_count": N,
    "test_baseline": N,
    "test_regression": false,
    "vhs_profiles_pass": 5,
    "vhs_profiles_total": 5,
    "visual_regressions": []
  }

## Logging
- All commands log to /tmp/gate3_*.log
- Summary JSON at /tmp/gate3_summary.json
- VHS suite outputs at /tmp/tui_inspector/suites/phase3_post_upgrade/

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] go build succeeds
- [ ] go vet clean (no warnings)
- [ ] Test count >= baseline (no regression)
- [ ] All 5 VHS profiles pass
- [ ] No visual regressions vs previous phase
- [ ] Summary JSON created with all metrics
- [ ] All logs archived

## Gate Policy
If ANY check fails, investigate and fix before proceeding to next phase.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":15,"created_at":"2026-03-20T22:29:53.016835262Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T00:24:37.114325421Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gate","phase-3","tui","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-7dnb7","depends_on_id":"br-obaln","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-20T22:33:54.109045610Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-7dnb7","depends_on_id":"br-phdna","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-20T22:34:54.992984241Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-7dnb7","depends_on_id":"br-zqcqd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-20T22:33:55.912758959Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-7h8it","title":"Attention Feed Phase 5: Validate the end-to-end operator loop and enforce rollout guardrails","description":"# Why this bead exists
The final question is not merely "did the code compile?" but "did ntm become a more agent-intuitive nervous system without drifting into a fake coordinator, and do the human-facing summary surfaces agree with that reality?" This bead is the capstone validation and rollout gate for the attention-feed initiative.

# Scope
1. Validate the implemented operator loop end to end:
   - cold start with snapshot
   - wait or attention against a known cursor
   - wake on meaningful change
   - inspect raw events only when needed
   - resync cleanly when replay is no longer possible
2. Confirm CLI, HTTP, SSE, and WebSocket parity where promised.
3. Confirm the discoverability surfaces (`--robot-help`, `--robot-capabilities`) accurately reflect reality.
4. Confirm dashboard and terse output reflect the same frontier as the operator-facing feed.
5. Confirm low-noise operator mode and high-fidelity debug mode both behave as intended.
6. Confirm the feature did not smuggle in a planner, second task graph, or hidden coordinator state.
7. Confirm both the deterministic unit/integration suite and the transcript-style e2e suite actually make failures diagnosable.
8. Record rollout guardrails in the implementation/docs/help output so future contributors understand the intended boundary of the feature.
9. Treat overlay-specific follow-on integration as separate work unless those beads are explicitly pulled into scope later; this gate is for the core attention-feed initiative.

# Validation checklist
- [ ] `go build ./cmd/ntm`
- [ ] `go test -short ./...`
- [ ] `go test -v ./e2e/...`
- [ ] relevant targeted package tests pass
- [ ] transcript-style e2e coverage for the new operator loop passes
- [ ] cursor expiry / resync behavior is clear and non-misleading
- [ ] operator loop feels simpler than repeated snapshot polling
- [ ] no schema drift between the promised contract and actual outputs
- [ ] no accidental architectural overreach into planning/coordinating logic
- [ ] help/capabilities/dashboard/terse output agree with the implemented command set
- [ ] operator/debug profile behavior is coherent and documented
- [ ] unit/integration logs and e2e artifacts are detailed enough to debug failures after the fact

# Artifact expectations
Closing this bead should leave behind a concise verification record identifying what commands/tests were run and where the most useful transcript or log artifacts can be found.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:22.027176806Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T09:44:21.359757149Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["attention-feed","gate","phase-5","robot","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-7h8it","depends_on_id":"br-9bmtl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:30.518584145Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-7h8it","depends_on_id":"br-auag6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:11:30.534838247Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-7h8it","depends_on_id":"br-mamo3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:46.125456143Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-7h8it","depends_on_id":"br-rkh17","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:45.685713666Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Bug fixes for channel lifecycle (swarm, webhook) and nil safety (assign) verified working.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:22.027176806Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T09:56:13.236851756Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T09:56:13.235775Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["attention-feed","gate","phase-5","robot","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-7h8it","depends_on_id":"br-9bmtl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:30.518584145Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-7h8it","depends_on_id":"br-auag6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:11:30.534838247Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-7h8it","depends_on_id":"br-mamo3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:46.125456143Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-7h8it","depends_on_id":"br-rkh17","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:45.685713666Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-7kigk","title":"Attention Feed Phase 3c: Evolve --robot-wait into a condition-driven attention gate","description":"# Why this bead exists
`internal/robot/wait.go` already offers useful waiting behavior, but it is currently narrower and more polling-oriented than what an operator LLM needs. This bead evolves wait into a condition-driven attention gate that can sleep efficiently until specific kinds of change occur.

# Mandatory condition set
Existing conditions to preserve:
- `idle`
- `complete`
- `generating`
- `healthy`

New attention-oriented conditions to add:
- `attention`
- `action_required`
- `mail_pending`
- `mail_ack_required`
- `stalled`
- `rate_limited`
- `context_hot`
- `reservation_conflict`
- `file_conflict`
- `session_changed`
- `pane_changed`
- `bead_orphaned` (only if implemented honestly)

# Scope
1. Add named conditions and keep them enumerable/documented.
2. Add `--since-cursor` support so waiters can bind themselves to a known observation frontier.
3. Preserve sensible timeout, transition, and error semantics.
4. Return a machine-usable wake payload that includes the matched condition, relevant cursor boundary/hand-off information, and enough detail for a caller to move into digest/events follow-up without guessing.
5. Ensure wait decisions consume the same normalized feed/signal pipeline rather than duplicating state logic.
6. Explicitly reject a general expression language unless a clear need emerges later.
7. Make honest degradation explicit for conditions that are partially observable or intentionally unsupported in v1.

# Guardrails
- Keep the interface teachable to an LLM.
- Avoid condition semantics that depend on undocumented background polling behavior.
- If a condition cannot be implemented honestly, narrow it rather than shipping a misleading version.

# Detailed verification expectations
- Synthetic feed tests for each new condition.
- Timeout tests and transition-boundary tests.
- Tests for wake payload shape, matched-condition reporting, and cursor handoff semantics.
- Tests for honest degradation when a condition is unavailable or only partially observable.
- Scenario transcripts for wait wakeups and timeouts using snapshot-provided cursors.
- Detailed logs in tests for matched condition, timeout path, observed cursor range, returned wake payload, and ignored competing signals.

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Existing wait functionality is preserved.
- [ ] The new condition set is available, documented, and consistent with the feed taxonomy.
- [ ] `--since-cursor` works as a real boundary, not a cosmetic flag.
- [ ] Wait responses expose machine-usable wake payloads and cursor handoff semantics.
- [ ] Timeouts and error payloads remain machine-usable.
- [ ] Condition behavior is covered by transcript-style scenarios and detailed logs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:21.152265573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T08:55:00.051078465Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T08:55:00.050088261Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["attention-feed","phase-3","robot","wait"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-7kigk","depends_on_id":"br-04qv0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:25.486200475Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-7kigk","depends_on_id":"br-3qs46","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:41.750052444Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-7kigk","depends_on_id":"br-89wvt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:43.271960296Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-7kigk","depends_on_id":"br-fsoxa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:25.820885564Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-7kigk","depends_on_id":"br-vdfjr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:41.383112878Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-7v4ns","title":"Attention Feed Phase 1b: Map existing bus/tracker/session signals into one normalized feed service","description":"# Why this bead exists
A journal alone is not useful if each producer speaks a different dialect. ntm already emits data from several places: event bus notifications, tracker state changes, session/pane lifecycle transitions, alerts, activity monitors, and other robot helpers. This bead makes those sources legible to a robot client through one internal mapping/query layer.

# Scope
1. Map existing tracker change types and bus events into the normalized envelope.
2. Decide which fields should be filled for each source: category, type, summary, details, source, and candidate `next_actions`.
3. Route session and pane lifecycle events through the same path used for replay and live subscription.
4. Create one shared query/subscription service so `--robot-events`, `--robot-digest`, wait conditions, and server streaming all consume the same underlying feed.
5. Keep raw output out of the high-level envelope unless it is genuinely required; the feed should tell an operator what changed, not dump pane text continuously.
6. Document unmapped or intentionally suppressed source events in code/tests so future contributors understand what was omitted and why.

# Design considerations
- Summary strings should be compact but concrete, because they are what an LLM will read most often.
- `details` should hold structured fields that later commands can use mechanically.
- `next_actions` must never point at imaginary commands; they should reference real robot-mode follow-ups such as `--robot-tail`, `--robot-context`, `--robot-status`, or the new feed surfaces.
- Avoid duplicating mapping logic in both CLI and server code; parity depends on one source of truth.

# Detailed verification expectations
- Mapping tests for tracker changes, bus events, session creation/removal, pane creation/removal, alerts, and activity changes.
- Tests that validate `next_actions` only reference real supported surfaces.
- Fixture coverage table that lists each input event family and the expected normalized output shape.
- Debug logging in tests for mapping misses or intentionally dropped events.

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Existing ntm signals are representable through one normalized mapping path.
- [ ] Replay and live subscription go through the same mapping logic.
- [ ] Event summaries/details are specific enough to guide follow-up inspection without requiring raw logs.
- [ ] The implementation does not fork into separate CLI-only and server-only schemas.
- [ ] Unmapped or intentionally omitted signals are explicit rather than accidental.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:19.637988898Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T03:11:04.602754573Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T03:11:04.602174267Z","close_reason":"Added shared AttentionFeed publish helpers for tracker/logged/bus sources, live bus subscription, and mapping/service tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["attention-feed","events","mapping","phase-1","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-7v4ns","depends_on_id":"br-xmmaj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:22.564595243Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-7zdzv","title":"PHASE 0: Capture TUI Baselines via VHS Inspector","description":"# Phase 0: Capture TUI Baselines

## Why This Must Come First
Every subsequent TUI change needs a visual reference point. Without baselines, we cannot
objectively verify that upgrades improve the UI without introducing regressions. The TUI
Inspector infrastructure already exists — we just need to run it and store the artifacts.

## What To Do

### Step 1: Run doctor check
  bash /dp/tui_inspector/scripts/doctor_tui_inspector.sh
Verify: vhs, ttyd, ffmpeg all present and functional. Log the output.

### Step 2: Build NTM binary
  go build -o /tmp/ntm_baseline ./cmd/ntm
Record the binary size and build time.

### Step 3: Capture all 5 profiles
  bash /dp/tui_inspector/scripts/run_ntm_tui_suite.sh \
    --profiles ntm-dashboard-empty,ntm-dashboard-help,ntm-dashboard-navigate,ntm-dashboard-wide,ntm-palette \
    --suite-name "baseline_pre_upgrade"
Log: each profile's exit code, video duration, snapshot status.

### Step 4: Run native VHS tapes
  cd /data/projects/ntm/testdata/vhs && for tape in *.tape; do vhs "$tape" 2>&1 | tee /tmp/vhs_${tape%.tape}.log; done

### Step 5: Run existing TUI unit tests as baseline
  go test -v -short ./internal/tui/... 2>&1 | tee /tmp/tui_tests_baseline.log
Record: test count, pass/fail, duration. This is the baseline test count we must not regress.

### Step 6: Archive baseline artifacts
Store in /tmp/tui_inspector/suites/baseline_pre_upgrade/
Create a manifest: baseline_manifest.json with binary size, test count, capture metadata.

## Unit Test Baseline
Run and record all existing tests:
  go test -v -count=1 -short ./internal/tui/... 2>&1 | tee /tmp/tui_test_baseline.log
  go test -v -count=1 -short ./internal/cli/... 2>&1 | tee /tmp/cli_test_baseline.log
Count passing tests. This number MUST NOT decrease in any subsequent phase.

## Logging
- Log doctor check output to /tmp/tui_inspector/baseline_doctor.log
- Log each VHS capture stdout/stderr to /tmp/tui_inspector/baseline_capture_*.log
- Log test run output to /tmp/tui_test_baseline.log
- Create /tmp/tui_inspector/baseline_summary.json with:
  { "binary_size": N, "test_count": N, "test_pass": N, "capture_count": 5, "all_pass": bool }

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 5 TUI Inspector profiles capture successfully (exit code 0 in run_meta.json)
- All 5 snapshots extracted (snapshot_exists: true in each run_meta.json)
- Suite manifest shows 5/5 passed
- All existing TUI unit tests pass
- Baseline artifacts archived and accessible
